comment_text.sn22.96:1.2: “That” (so) refers back to an introductory passage that has been elided.
comment_text.sn22.96:3.1: These details are greatly expanded in DN 17, where the Buddha names himself Mahāsudassana.
comment_text.sn22.96:3.4: “Chamber” is kūṭāgāra, literally “building with peaked roof”. Normally this means “bungalow”, but in a similar description at MN 37:8.9 we hear of a “palace” with a hundred “towers” (niyyūha), each of which has seven hundred “chambers”. Thus the kūṭāgāra must be a structure many of which can belong to a palace, namely “chamber”, while byūha (spelled viyūha at DN 17:2.5.3) must have the same meaning as niyyūha, i.e. “tower”.
comment_text.sn22.96:3.5: “Silver” is omitted by mistake in Mahāsaṅgīti edition.
comment_text.sn22.96:3.6: Hema (“snow gold”) evokes the golden radiance of a snowy mountain in the dawn light, a blessing of the gods that, it may easily be imagined, leaves its traces in the gold found in abundance in its streams.
comment_text.sn22.96:3.8: Not the British car of the same name. The chariot is the source of victory in battle.
comment_text.sn22.96:3.12: Dukūla (“muslin”) is a delicate cloth produced from the inner bark of a tree. | Sandāna is a tether or “halter” for cows or horses (Rig Veda 1.162.8) or even for binding foes (Atharva Veda 6.103).
comment_text.sn22.96:4.8: Readings here and at DN 17:2.15.8 are confused, and further complicated at Tha Ap 411:4.1. I read  velāsikā due to the passage’s similarity to DN 23:15.4, where a “coquette” (literally, “party girl”) is also serving a royal. I believe the variant velāmikā is a ghost word contaminated from AN 9.20:4.1.

root_text.sn22.96:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.96:1.3: “atthi nu kho, bhante, kiñci rūpaṁ yaṁ rūpaṁ niccaṁ dhuvaṁ sassataṁ avipariṇāmadhammaṁ sassatisamaṁ tatheva ṭhassati?
root_text.sn22.96:1.4: Atthi nu kho, bhante, kāci vedanā yā vedanā niccā dhuvā sassatā avipariṇāmadhammā sassatisamaṁ tatheva ṭhassati?
root_text.sn22.96:1.5: Atthi nu kho, bhante, kāci saññā yā saññā …pe…
root_text.sn22.96:1.6: atthi nu kho, bhante, keci saṅkhārā ye saṅkhārā niccā dhuvā sassatā avipariṇāmadhammā sassatisamaṁ tatheva ṭhassanti?
root_text.sn22.96:1.7: Atthi nu kho, bhante, kiñci viññāṇaṁ, yaṁ viññāṇaṁ niccaṁ dhuvaṁ sassataṁ avipariṇāmadhammaṁ sassatisamaṁ tatheva ṭhassatī”ti?
root_text.sn22.96:1.8: “Natthi kho, bhikkhu, kiñci rūpaṁ, yaṁ rūpaṁ niccaṁ dhuvaṁ sassataṁ avipariṇāmadhammaṁ sassatisamaṁ tatheva ṭhassati.
root_text.sn22.96:1.12: kiñci viññāṇaṁ, yaṁ viññāṇaṁ niccaṁ dhuvaṁ sassataṁ avipariṇāmadhammaṁ sassatisamaṁ tatheva ṭhassatī”ti.
root_text.sn22.96:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā parittaṁ gomayapiṇḍaṁ pāṇinā gahetvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn22.96:2.2: “ettakopi kho, bhikkhu, attabhāvapaṭilābho natthi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo sassatisamaṁ tatheva ṭhassati.
root_text.sn22.96:2.3: Ettako cepi, bhikkhu, attabhāvapaṭilābho abhavissa nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo, nayidaṁ brahmacariyavāso paññāyetha sammā dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn22.96:2.4: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhu, ettakopi attabhāvapaṭilābho natthi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo, tasmā brahmacariyavāso paññāyati sammā dukkhakkhayāya.
root_text.sn22.96:3.1: Bhūtapubbāhaṁ, bhikkhu, rājā ahosiṁ khattiyo muddhāvasitto.
root_text.sn22.96:3.2: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu, rañño sato khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa caturāsītinagarasahassāni ahesuṁ kusāvatīrājadhānippamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.3: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu, rañño sato khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa caturāsītipāsādasahassāni ahesuṁ dhammapāsādappamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.4: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu, rañño sato khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa caturāsītikūṭāgārasahassāni ahesuṁ mahābyūhakūṭāgārappamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.5: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu, rañño sato khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa caturāsītipallaṅkasahassāni ahesuṁ dantamayāni sāramayāni sovaṇṇamayāni goṇakatthatāni paṭikatthatāni paṭalikatthatāni kadalimigapavarapaccattharaṇāni sauttaracchadāni ubhatolohitakūpadhānāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.6: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu, rañño sato khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa caturāsītināgasahassāni ahesuṁ sovaṇṇālaṅkārāni sovaṇṇaddhajāni hemajālapaṭicchannāni uposathanāgarājappamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.7: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu, rañño sato khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa caturāsītiassasahassāni ahesuṁ sovaṇṇālaṅkārāni sovaṇṇaddhajāni hemajālapaṭicchannāni valāhakaassarājappamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.8: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu, rañño sato khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa caturāsītirathasahassāni ahesuṁ sovaṇṇālaṅkārāni sovaṇṇaddhajāni hemajālapaṭicchannāni vejayantarathappamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.9: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu, rañño sato khattiyassa muddhāvasittassa caturāsītimaṇisahassāni ahesuṁ maṇiratanappamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.10: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu …pe… caturāsītiitthisahassāni ahesuṁ subhaddādevippamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.11: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu …pe… caturāsītikhattiyasahassāni ahesuṁ anuyantāni pariṇāyakaratanappamukhāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.12: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu …pe… caturāsītidhenusahassāni ahesuṁ dukūlasandānāni kaṁsūpadhāraṇāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.13: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu …pe… caturāsītivatthakoṭisahassāni ahesuṁ khomasukhumāni koseyyasukhumāni kambalasukhumāni kappāsikasukhumāni.
root_text.sn22.96:3.14: Tassa mayhaṁ, bhikkhu …pe… caturāsītithālipākasahassāni ahesuṁ; sāyaṁ pātaṁ bhattābhihāro abhihariyittha.
root_text.sn22.96:4.1: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā nagarasahassānaṁ ekaññeva taṁ nagaraṁ hoti yamahaṁ tena samayena ajjhāvasāmi—kusāvatī rājadhānī.
root_text.sn22.96:4.2: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā pāsādasahassānaṁ ekoyeva so pāsādo hoti yamahaṁ tena samayena ajjhāvasāmi—dhammo pāsādo.
root_text.sn22.96:4.3: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā kūṭāgārasahassānaṁ ekaññeva taṁ kūṭāgāraṁ hoti yamahaṁ tena samayena ajjhāvasāmi—mahābyūhaṁ kūṭāgāraṁ.
root_text.sn22.96:4.4: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā pallaṅkasahassānaṁ ekoyeva so pallaṅko hoti yamahaṁ tena samayena paribhuñjāmi—dantamayo vā sāramayo vā sovaṇṇamayo vā rūpiyamayo vā.
root_text.sn22.96:4.5: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā nāgasahassānaṁ ekoyeva so nāgo hoti yamahaṁ tena samayena abhiruhāmi—uposatho nāgarājā.
root_text.sn22.96:4.6: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā assasahassānaṁ ekoyeva so asso hoti yamahaṁ tena samayena abhiruhāmi—valāhako assarājā.
root_text.sn22.96:4.7: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā rathasahassānaṁ ekoyeva so ratho hoti yamahaṁ tena samayena abhiruhāmi—vejayanto ratho.
root_text.sn22.96:4.8: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā itthisahassānaṁ ekāyeva sā itthī hoti yā maṁ tena samayena paccupaṭṭhāti—khattiyānī vā velāmikā vā.
root_text.sn22.96:4.9: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā vatthakoṭisahassānaṁ ekaññeva taṁ vatthayugaṁ hoti yamahaṁ tena samayena paridahāmi—khomasukhumaṁ vā koseyyasukhumaṁ vā kambalasukhumaṁ vā kappāsikasukhumaṁ vā.
root_text.sn22.96:4.10: Tesaṁ kho pana, bhikkhu, caturāsītiyā thālipākasahassānaṁ ekoyeva so thālipāko hoti yato nāḷikodanaparamaṁ bhuñjāmi tadupiyañca sūpeyyaṁ.
root_text.sn22.96:4.11: Iti kho, bhikkhu, sabbe te saṅkhārā atītā niruddhā vipariṇatā.
root_text.sn22.96:4.15: Yāvañcidaṁ, bhikkhu, alameva sabbasaṅkhāresu nibbindituṁ, alaṁ virajjituṁ, alaṁ vimuccitun”ti.

translation_text.sn22.96:1.2: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn22.96:1.3: “Sir, is there any form at all that’s permanent, everlasting, eternal, imperishable, and will last forever and ever?
translation_text.sn22.96:1.7: consciousness at all that’s permanent, everlasting, eternal, imperishable, and will last forever and ever?”
translation_text.sn22.96:1.8: “Mendicant, there is no form at all that’s permanent, everlasting, eternal, imperishable, and will last forever and ever.
translation_text.sn22.96:1.12: consciousness at all that’s permanent, everlasting, eternal, imperishable, and will last forever and ever.”
translation_text.sn22.96:2.1: Then the Buddha, picking up a lump of cow dung, said to the mendicants:
translation_text.sn22.96:2.2: “There’s not even this much of any incarnation that’s permanent, everlasting, eternal, imperishable, and will last forever and ever.
translation_text.sn22.96:2.3: If there were, this living of the spiritual life for the complete ending of suffering would not be found.
translation_text.sn22.96:2.4: But since there isn’t, this living of the spiritual life for the complete ending of suffering is found.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.1: Once upon a time I was an anointed aristocratic king.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.2: I had 84,000 cities, with the capital Kusāvatī the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.3: I had 84,000 palaces, with the Palace of Principle the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.4: I had 84,000 chambers, with the Chamber of the Grand Tower the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.5: I had 84,000 couches made of ivory or heartwood or gold or silver, spread with woolen covers—shag-piled or embroidered with flowers—and spread with a fine deer hide, with a canopy above and red pillows at both ends.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.6: I had 84,000 bull elephants with gold adornments and banners, covered with snow gold netting, with the royal bull elephant named Sabbath the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.7: I had 84,000 horses with gold adornments and banners, covered with snow gold netting, with the royal steed named Thundercloud the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.8: I had 84,000 chariots with gold adornments and banners, covered with snow gold netting, with the chariot named Triumph the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.9: I had 84,000 jewels, with the jewel-treasure the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.10: I had 84,000 women, with Queen Subhaddā the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.11: I had 84,000 aristocrat vassals, with the commander-treasure the foremost.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.12: I had 84,000 milk cows with muslin halter and bronze pails.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.13: I had 8,400,000,000 delicate cloths of linen, cotton, silk, and wool.
translation_text.sn22.96:3.14: I had 84,000 servings of food, which were presented to me as offerings at daybreak and dusk.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.1: Of those 84,000 cities, I only stayed in one, the capital Kusāvatī.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.2: Of those 84,000 palaces, I only dwelt in one, the Palace of Principle.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.3: Of those 84,000 chambers, I only dwelt in one, the  Chamber of the Grand Tower.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.4: Of those 84,000 couches, I only used one, made of ivory or heartwood or gold or silver.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.5: Of those 84,000 bull elephants, I only rode one, the royal bull elephant named Sabbath.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.6: Of those 84,000 horses, I only rode one, the royal horse named Thundercloud.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.7: Of those 84,000 chariots, I only rode one, the chariot named Triumph.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.8: Of those 84,000 women, I was only served by one, an aristocrat maiden or a coquette.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.9: Of those 8,400,000,000 cloths, I only wore one pair, made of delicate linen, cotton, silk, and wool.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.10: Of those 84,000 servings of food, I only had one, eating at most a cup of rice with suitable sauce.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.11: And so all those conditioned phenomena have passed, ceased, and perished.
translation_text.sn22.96:4.15: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”

variant_text.sn22.96:4.10: tadupiyañca sūpeyyaṁ → sūpabyañjanaṁ (sya-all, km); tadūpiyañca supeyyaṁ (pts1ed)
